mappings.rationale: The primary VSI for this section, directly covering growth, differentiation, morphogenesis (B.1-3), blastula/gastrulation (D.2.a), Hox genes in organ formation (D.3), and identical twins (F.4).
Directly addresses cell differentiation (B.3), biological regeneration (F.1), and development in vitro including cell and tissue cultures (F.5).
Explains the cell cycle, cell growth (B.1), the nucleus and nuclear envelope during division, and the cellular basis of differentiation (B.3).
Treats plant development (C), including pollination mechanisms (C.2.b), seed germination and dispersal (C.3.b), and the sporophyte plant body (C.4).
Addresses genetic control and integration of development (B.4) including gene expression patterns, molecular biology of differentiation, and regulation during morphogenesis.
Details growth hormone and insulin-like growth factor in controlling growth (B.1), along with hormonal regulation of postembryonic development and metamorphosis (D.4).
Relevant to biological malformation (F.3) where control of cell growth and differentiation fails, leading to abnormal development.
Elucidates molecular mechanisms of gene regulation underlying the control and integration of developmental processes (B.4), including transcription factors and signaling cascades.
Surveys the diversity of embryonic development and organ formation across invertebrate phyla (D.2-3), including variations in gastrulation and metamorphosis.
Directly relevant to aging and decline (E) including senescence (E.1), causes of aging (E.2), life expectancy, and the duration of life (E.3).
Describes mammalian embryonic adaptations including placental development (D.2.b), postembryonic growth, and senescence in mammals (E.1).
Highlights amphibian metamorphosis as a paradigmatic example of postembryonic transformation (D.4) and developmental adaptation to aquatic and terrestrial life.
Shows how symbiotic interactions with microbiomes influence biological development and growth processes in both plants and animals (B.4).
